llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm available: factset_ai_assisted, which represents reports using the AI-powered FactSet Mercury platform
llm_extracted.notes: Re-read identified key statistics missed in prior pass. Table 1 Panel A reports the sample mean of Accuracy as -0.742 with SD 1.363 (N=50742 total sample). Table 7 Panel C reports the DiD regression result for Accuracy: FactSet×Post coefficient = -0.438 (t = -3.62, p < 0.01). The matched sample after PSM and entropy balancing has N_treated=14182 and N_control=28364 (Table 6). The control mean is -0.742 (from Table 1). The AI effect is a decrease of 0.438 (making forecast errors larger, i.e., accuracy worse), so m_ai = -0.742 - 0.438 = -1.180. The abstract states 'forecast errors rise by 59%' which equals 0.438/0.742. The outcome is defined as negative of absolute forecast error, so larger (less negative) values are better accuracy. The t-statistic -3.62 is reported but df is not explicitly stated. The p-value is reported as inequality p < 0.01, not exact value. SD for the AI arm is not separately reported. Using Level A (means + SD for control + n for both arms) and Level C (t-statistic without df).
source_quotes: Accuracy: Forecast accuracy, measured as the negative of the absolute difference between the analyst's earnings per share (EPS) forecast and the EPS realized subsequently (Clement, 1999). [...] Panel A of Table 1 shows summary statistics on these variables. The average forecast error is $0.74. (Table 1, page 17)
Variable N Mean SD Min p25 p50 p75 Max [...] Accuracy 50742 -0.742 1.363 -12.660 -0.750 -0.260 -0.090 0.000 (Table 1 Panel A, page 49)
Table 6 reports the covariate moments—mean, standard deviation, and skewness—for the treated group (N = 14,182) and the matched control group (N = 28,364). (page 27)

Panel C shifts the focus from report richness to report quality, proxied by forecast timeliness and accuracy as defined in Section 3.2. [...] In contrast, the second column shows that the coefficient on FactSet × Post is negative and significant at the 1% level for forecast accuracy. In economic terms, forecast errors increase by roughly $0.44, or 59% (= 0.438/0.742) of the sample average. (page 29)
Table 7 Panel C: Report quality, main results [...] Accuracy: FactSet×Post -0.438*** (-3.62) (Table 7, page 57)

computed.notes: Re-read identified key statistics missed in prior pass. Table 1 Panel A reports the sample mean of Accuracy as -0.742 with SD 1.363 (N=50742 total sample). Table 7 Panel C reports the DiD regression result for Accuracy: FactSet×Post coefficient = -0.438 (t = -3.62, p < 0.01). The matched sample after PSM and entropy balancing has N_treated=14182 and N_control=28364 (Table 6). The control mean is -0.742 (from Table 1). The AI effect is a decrease of 0.438 (making forecast errors larger, i.e., accuracy worse), so m_ai = -0.742 - 0.438 = -1.180. The abstract states 'forecast errors rise by 59%' which equals 0.438/0.742. The outcome is defined as negative of absolute forecast error, so larger (less negative) values are better accuracy. The t-statistic -3.62 is reported but df is not explicitly stated. The p-value is reported as inequality p < 0.01, not exact value. SD for the AI arm is not separately reported. Using Level A (means + SD for control + n for both arms) and Level C (t-statistic without df).
